assertions.reason: EditorView tests confirm the first panel renders '01 Script' with 'draft · markdown' and the second renders '02 Preview' with 'live render'.
MarkdownEditor unit tests verify textarea value mirrors scriptStore content, typing updates the store, the placeholder mentions markdown, and spellcheck is enabled.
MarkdownPreview unit tests cover plain text, h1, strong, em, unordered lists, GFM strikethrough, raw HTML, gp-prose wrapper, and custom className support.
EditorView tests verify Words, Chars, and Read time stat blocks render, update when content changes, and show F7 Play, F6 Edit, and Esc Exit keyboard hints.
SettingsModal tests verify opening the modal, defaulting to Appearance, rendering all four tabs, and closing via X or backdrop while ignoring clicks inside the modal body.
HotkeyRecorder tests confirm display/record toggling plus normalization for F-keys, compound modifiers, uppercase letters, arrows, Space, Escape, shifted digits/punctuation, and modifier-only guards.
Static analysis confirms EditorView imports MarkdownEditor, MarkdownPreview, and SettingsModal from separate modules, and all five required source/test files exist as standalone files.
Static analysis plus IPC contract tests confirm frontend command strings match Rust invoke registrations, and settings persistence uses LazyStore from @tauri-apps/plugin-store rather than custom IPC.

commandsRun.command: npm run test:frontend -- src/editor/EditorView.test.tsx src/editor/MarkdownEditor.test.tsx src/editor/MarkdownPreview.test.tsx src/settings/SettingsModal.test.tsx src/settings/HotkeyRecorder.test.tsx
commandsRun.observation: Targeted Vitest run passed: 5 selected files, 100 tests total, 0 failures. React act(...) warnings appeared during the VAL-EDIT-010 reactive stats test but did not fail the run.

commandsRun.command: PowerShell static analysis over EditorView.tsx, settingsStore.ts, ipc.ts, src-tauri/src/lib.rs, and HotkeyRecorder.tsx to emit mission evidence JSON
commandsRun.observation: Confirmed module/test-file existence, EditorView imports, LazyStore usage, frontend IPC strings, matching Rust invoke registrations, and hotkey normalization helpers.

frictions.description: The targeted Vitest run emitted React act(...) warnings for the VAL-EDIT-010 content-change rerender assertion in EditorView.test.tsx.
frictions.resolution: No workaround was required because the warning did not affect the pass/fail result; recorded in evidence for synthesis.
